plainEnglish: At Level AA, pre-recorded video with audio must include an audio description track—narration during natural pauses that describes important visual details. Unlike 1.2.3 (which also allows a text-only alternative), this criterion specifically requires audio description, so blind users get the visual information through sound.
whyItMatters: Blind users miss critical on-screen action and text when a video lacks an audio description track narrating the visuals.
quickCheck: Confirm the video offers an audio-described version or a <track kind="descriptions">, and verify it by listening with the screen off.
commonMistakes: Treating audio description as optional because captions already exist.
Adding descriptions that talk over the dialogue instead of fitting them into pauses.
Providing only a text transcript, which doesn't satisfy 1.2.5's specific requirement for audio description.

codeExamples.label: Video offering an audio-description track
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/feature.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/feature.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
  <track kind="descriptions" src="/feature.desc.vtt" srcLang="en" label="Audio description" />
</video>

codeExamples.label: Video with no audio description
codeExamples.code:
<video controls src="/feature.mp4">
  <track kind="captions" src="/feature.en.vtt" srcLang="en" label="English" default />
</video>
